entries.title: Foundational Keller collision
entries.scope: A concrete Keller map with the asserted collision and noninjectivity properties.

entries.title: Tangent-map core and weighted suspension
entries.scope: Every polynomial core and admissible weighted seed; inverse pencil, Jacobian/critical divisor, discriminant normalization, Hessian Fitting divisor, incidence normal form, and suspension comparison.

entries.title: Cancellation construction
entries.scope: All cancellation parameters m,r: universal Jacobian, polynomiality criterion, unique parameter-root jet, reconstruction degree, and symbolic collision.

entries.title: Complete canonical boundary exhaustion
entries.scope: The complete codimension-one canonical boundary list for the cancellation construction.

entries.title: Unconditional cancellation thick contact
entries.scope: All m,r thick contacts, with exact scheme intersection and nilpotency index.
supersedes: conditional contact-resultant input for the total contact length

entries.title: Stable normalization functoriality
entries.scope: Polynomial stabilization preserves normalization, boundary primes and (e,f), scheme intersections and nilpotency indices, Fitting ideals, node pairings, and conductors.

entries.title: Cancellation reconstruction residue and parameter faithfulness
entries.scope: The marked reconstruction open; the cancellation reconstruction residue q is invariant and separates parameter roots.

entries.title: Degreewise finite count
entries.scope: Every fixed degree N in the stated cancellation family; finite stable multiplicity from boundary data, thick contact, and parameter-root counting.

entries.title: Marked Hessian-divisor moduli
entries.scope: The quotient of (P^1; div(H''), 0, infinity) has stable-moduli dimension N-3; its generic map is unramified and has exact rerooting degree N-2. Node pairing and conductor are not used.

entries.title: Generic affine-mark faithfulness and marked normalization
entries.scope: On the Hessian-clean open, the marked Hessian divisor plus one affine root sheet recovers the seed exactly; after shrinking, the seed open normalizes the marked image and forgetting the sheet is finite étale of degree N-2.

entries.title: Hurwitz--LL compactification of rerooting
entries.scope: Internal admissible-cover/Hurwitz--LL compactification theorem; repository proof is complete but externally unreviewed.

entries.title: Stacky marked extension
entries.scope: The selected root extends tautologically on the marked admissible-cover stack; the marked-to-unmarked morphism is finite, representable, and étale of degree N-2, and its normalized Stein contractions, completed root-cover charts, and conductor squares agree with the repository constructions at arbitrary simultaneous multicluster collisions.

entries.title: Coarse affine-mark descent
entries.scope: The closure of the distinguished degree-one regular-reconstruction component is isomorphic to the normal marked coarse compactification and has a unique specialization over every DVR limit.  At a collision of multiplicity mu the coarse marked chart is the universal monic-root incidence with special fiber k[T]/(T^mu), so pair, triple, and arbitrary simultaneous collisions have one geometric coarse mark.

entries.title: Formal orbit triviality
entries.scope: Finite-order deformations modulo unrestricted polynomial source automorphisms are formally orbit-trivial.

entries.title: Formal LR target-lift reduction
entries.scope: Every based formal target automorphism B has a unique formal source lift delta_F(B) with B composed with F equal to F composed with delta_F(B); if F_t=F composed with alpha_t, every formal LR trivialization is uniquely A_t=alpha_t^(-1) composed with delta_F(B_t)^(-1). Thus all lower source jets are forced by the target jets. At order two the coefficient class is the explicit quadratic Theta_2(W_1), while logarithmic coordinates split it into an affine-linear bracket class Xi_2(Y_1) and one known quadratic coordinate-reconstruction term.
supersedes: independent lower source and target jet search in the LR strictness problems

entries.title: Filtered-coset BCH descent lemma
entries.scope: Formal LR source representatives form the right coset alpha^(-1)H_F of the lifted target subgroup. Excess degree makes the pre-Lie and BCH operations filtered. On the homogeneous-space quotient the order-two class is [-X_2+{X_1,D_1}/2]; under Rees-strict specialization its weight-zero part depends only on the weight-zero target direction, so opposite nonzero weights first occur in mixed BCH words at order three. The concrete OP-LR-REES application is reduced to linear Rees strictness of the target-lift coset.
supersedes: pair-by-pair opposite-weight expansion at order two

entries.title: Target-lift second fundamental form
entries.scope: The symmetric pre-Lie defect of the target lift is II_F(Y_1,Y_2)=-(DF)^(-1)D^2F[ell_F(Y_1),ell_F(Y_2)]. After strict linear specialization, its initial normal class is exactly the quadratic Rees-strictness obstruction. In the torus-equivariant problem, all opposite-weight order-two effects are the invariant-module maps II_(F,p,-p) into the weight-zero normal module.
supersedes: arbitrary-coefficient formulation of opposite-weight order-two terms

entries.title: Minimal quadratic Rees-torsion witness
entries.scope: For F_2 the unique constant opposite-weight target pair (partial_B,partial_C), of weights (1,-1), has lift degrees (19,31) and second-fundamental-form degree 49. Its third invariant-ring-saturated normal residue is -30(4896u^5-25092u^4+15232u^3-1887u^2+126u-21)/7, with nonzero associated-graded symbol -146880u^5/7 in gr(R/(gamma)). Thus quadratic target-lift Rees strictness fails at the smallest target degree and smallest nonzero weight; linear target-lift Rees strictness remains separate.
supersedes: vanishing branch of the quadratic target-lift strictness dichotomy

entries.title: All-degree rank-two residue and symplectic descent
entries.scope: All degrees in the rank-two residue construction, including uniform generic/exceptional charts and symplectic descent.
supersedes: faithfulness as a prerequisite for the principal rank-two dimension theorem

entries.title: Rank-two parameter faithfulness
entries.scope: The ordinary boundary-clean open in every degree; the affine root mark separates the rank-two parameters.

entries.title: Generic nodal-cuspidal discriminant geometry
entries.scope: proved on a nonempty open of normalized admissible degree-n seeds for every n >= 3

entries.title: Universal symmetric monodromy
entries.scope: Every characteristic-zero polynomial H of degree n >= 2: Gal(H(W)-sW+t/k(s,t)) and its geometric monodromy group are S_n; for n >= 3 the generic inverse cover has trivial deck group.
dependencies: external: Zariski--Nagata purity and triviality of finite etale covers of affine space

entries.title: Incidence-cover deck and pencil-symmetry classification
entries.scope: For every characteristic-zero polynomial H of degree N >= 3, the two-parameter incidence cover H(W)-sW+t=0 has trivial target-fixed deck group. Its pencil-compatible affine self-equivalences are exactly the affine stabilizer of the effective Hessian divisor div(H''), hence are cyclic in the centered finite-symmetry case and G_m only for a centered monomial; the corresponding normalized admissible-seed and coarse-orbifold strata are explicit.
supersedes: conflation of target-fixed deck transformations, pencil self-equivalences, and vertical imprimitivity

entries.title: Exact geometric-degree spectrum
entries.scope: The geometric degrees of noninvertible Keller maps A^3_C -> A^3_C are exactly 3,4,5,...; maps of different degrees are stably polynomially left--right inequivalent.
dependencies: external: birational and Galois cases of the Jacobian theorem

entries.title: All-degree complete rational fibers
entries.scope: For every N >= 3, an explicit weighted Keller map over Q has a complete fiber of exactly N distinct rational points and an open real target neighborhood with N real sheets.

entries.title: Exact real-sheet spectrum
entries.scope: For every N >= 3, the explicit rational weighted Keller map F_N realizes exactly the parity-compatible counts N,N-2,...,N mod 2 on nonempty complete regular real target chambers; every count has a rational witness, the minimum is 0 in even degree and 1 in odd degree, and a vertical fold chain realizes every wall crossing.

entries.title: Canonical-family image and nonproperness
entries.scope: proved for H_d(W)=W^d(1-W) for every d >= 2, including the exact affine image and boundary fibers

entries.title: Omitted-value classification
entries.scope: complete finite classifier for every fixed seed, with a closed classification for primitives having two additional simple zeros

entries.title: Unique omitted-value lemma
entries.scope: Every normalized admissible seed of inverse degree n >= 3 over an algebraically closed field of characteristic zero has at most one omitted inverse-pencil value; consequently the exact full-contact strata form a disjoint stratification of the nonsurjective locus.

entries.title: Abstract threshold contact-atom schema
entries.scope: For every r >= 2, a reconstruction problem satisfying the stated exact-threshold, collision, irreducibility, and finite-recovery hypotheses has primitive components indexed by partitions using r,...,2r-1; dominant dimensions are a one-row integer program, component counts and standard codimensions are quasipolynomial, and bounded-difference Mason separation has an explicit strict range.

entries.title: Full-contact strata and exceptional components
entries.scope: proved in characteristic zero for every full-contact partition: nonemptiness, dimensions, exact closure order, irreducible exceptional components, and set-theoretic intersections

entries.title: Contact-atom principle and omitted-value phase diagram
entries.scope: proved for every normalized exceptional seed locus in characteristic zero: maximal contact types use only multiplicities two and three, the nonsurjective locus has dimension floor(N/2)-1 and codimension floor((N-3)/2), generic quartics are exceptional, and generic seeds are surjective for every N >= 5
supersedes: Mason--Stothers as the source of the two-three classification

entries.title: Exceptional-component normalization
entries.scope: proved for every maximal partition 2^a 3^b in characteristic zero, with an explicit smooth normalization and a generic branch-fiber formula over every collision stratum

entries.title: Generic degree-twelve local singularity
entries.scope: proved etale-locally on a dense open of the (6,6) collision stratum; the two branches are quadratically tangent, with transverse-slice Artin length and conductor colength four
supersedes: nodal model for the generic degree-twelve branch intersection

entries.title: Enumerative skeleton of the nonsurjective locus
entries.scope: Every inverse degree N >= 3: period-six component count, refined dimension and codimension series, interval-controlled set-theoretic component intersections, binomial normalization-branch profiles, the pre-projection coincident-root Chow package, the exact normalized-seed projective degree deg C_(a,b)=binom(a+b,a)(2^a 3^b-a-b-1), and all source-target graph multidegrees and graph Chow classes. The unique boundary base point changes only the top target degree, by a weighted local Segre correction. The local correction lemma extends to every finite labelled product of multiplicity atoms at least two, with length (ell+1)!/prod a_i! and a conditional global moving-degree formula; nonminimal component-intersection multiplicities remain excluded.

entries.title: Weighted minimal-intersection partition lattice
entries.scope: proved in characteristic zero for every pair of distinct atomic components: the unique minimal common coarsening is 6^t 3^min(b,d) 2^min(a,c), its generic transverse algebra is a product of t dual-number collision blocks and has length 2^t; the complete component-pair and first normalization-self-pair census is recorded through degree twenty

entries.title: Degree-eighteen ordered triple algebra and conductor equalizer
entries.scope: On a nonempty open of the ordered (6,6,6) collision chart of C_(3,4), the three normalization branches have pairwise transverse length four and ordered-triple transverse algebra k[e_1,e_2,e_3]/(e_1^2,e_2^2,e_3^2) of length eight, with nonzero cubic socle. The completed three-branch Cech equalizer is exact there; an exact excess-conormal map of ranks 16 to 10 has maximal minor 2401/256, and simultaneous complete-intersection generators give conductor (w_i,e_i^2,e_j^2e_k^2) on each sheet.
supersedes: pairwise-only description of the degree-eighteen three-sheet face

entries.title: Effective finite-field Chebotarev and fiber fingerprints
entries.scope: unconditional for every admissible number-field seed outside its explicit bad ideal; every good reduction retains geometric and arithmetic S_n monodromy, every prescribed squarefree factorization type occurs above an effective uniform threshold, fixed-point counts satisfy an effective Poisson(1) limit, and a deterministic modular witness generator is provided

entries.title: Arithmetic density of exceptional seeds
entries.scope: For every fixed N >= 4 outside a finite degreewise set of characteristics, every geometrically integral omitted-partition normalization of dimension d has q^d + O(q^(d-1/2)) rational points; every atomic component has leading constant one. For N >= 5 the nonsurjective normalized seed density is q^(-floor((N-3)/2))(1+O(q^(-1/2))). Jointly prescribing a squarefree Frobenius type mu multiplies the main term by |C_mu|/N!, and all counts transport unchanged to the optimal Gaussian mixed-moment coordinates.

entries.title: Adelic realization of complete Keller fibers
entries.scope: For every N >= 3, every parity-compatible real root count, and every finite collection of squarefree factorization partitions at good primes admitting local witnesses, one rational target of the explicit F_N realizes all conditions simultaneously, with a constructive denominator-one CRT lift and an explicit height bound from any certified real chamber box. Every prescribed partition is guaranteed at sufficiently large good primes; adding one local N-cycle produces a degree-N number field of the chosen signature, with arbitrarily many further unramified splitting conditions.
supersedes: separate real and modular fiber witnesses

entries.title: Hasse-principle failure for a complete Keller fiber
entries.scope: One explicit degree-eight weighted Keller map over Q with determinant -38 has the integral target (12138,-308652,1) whose complete regular fiber is Spec(Q[W]/(P)): it has no Q-point, exactly six real points, and at least one Q_p-point for every prime p. The intersective polynomial P has an integral root modulo every positive integer, and the C=1 reconstruction proves that no boundary point contributes an additional preimage.

entries.title: Stratified adelic seed-and-fiber engineering
entries.scope: For any locally closed normalized seed stratum having a rational affine chart, one rational seed and target simultaneously realize a prescribed nonempty real root chamber and finitely many squarefree modular factorization types whenever compatible local points exist on that chart. This covers omitted-component membership or nonmembership and exact Hessian-symmetry conditions on rational charts; geometric nonemptiness of their intersection remains a separate hypothesis. One exact type-(3,2) quintic seed with trivial Hessian stabilizer realizes all three quintic signatures while retaining a 5-cycle at 7 and type (2,2,1) at 11.

entries.title: Explicit quartic weighted geometry
entries.scope: Worked quartic tangent-map model and independent regression for W1.
supersedes: bounded quartic seed scan as theorem evidence

entries.title: External quartic-island identification
entries.scope: Audit of the pinned external quartic-map classification against the repository normal form.
supersedes: treating the three quartic islands as unrelated constructions

entries.title: Scheme-theoretic decorated-normalization invariant
entries.scope: The intrinsic finite decorated-normalization cover with full Fitting, node-pairing, and conductor data; this is a stronger refinement of D1/F2, not an input to their generic moduli proof.

entries.title: Cancellation contact resultant
entries.scope: Every m>=1 for the complete resultant columns r=1,2,3,4.

entries.title: Fifth cancellation contact-resultant column
entries.scope: Every m>=1 in the complete resultant column r=5.

entries.title: Diagonal truncated-binomial irreducibility
entries.scope: The cancellation parameter polynomial is irreducible for every r>=1 in the six columns 1<=m<=6, and for every fixed m it is irreducible for all sufficiently large r.
dependencies: external: prime number theorem and Rohrbach--Weis explicit prime-interval theorem

entries.title: Arithmetic all-r contact-resultant ranges
entries.scope: The cancellation contact resultant is nonzero for every r>=1 in the six columns 1<=m<=6, for all sufficiently large r at each fixed m, and throughout every other proved parameter-irreducibility range.
supersedes: treating parameter irreducibility as merely finite contact-resultant evidence

entries.title: Sixth contact-resultant endpoint reduction
entries.scope: For r=6 the common-root equations reduce exactly to z-degrees five and six; after removing the excluded factor (y-1)^7, their z-resultant has y-degree 29 and m-degree 90 and is nonzero at y=1. This is a bounded-degree reduction, not uniform nonvanishing.
supersedes: raw degree-6m Sylvester-resultant formulation for the first open column

entries.title: Sixth contact-resultant asymptotic branches
entries.scope: For r=6 all 29 endpoint-eliminant branches satisfy y=1+c/m+O(m^-2), with squarefree nonzero algebraic c and a uniquely reconstructed algebraic limiting z. Lindemann--Weierstrass separates z from exp(c), so the contact resultant is nonzero for all sufficiently large m. No explicit threshold is claimed.
supersedes: whole-eliminant Schur--Cohn continuation for the r=6 asymptotic tail

entries.title: Degree-five decorated-moduli specialization
entries.scope: Worked N=5 specialization on a nonempty open Lambda^o=Lambda intersected with the ordinary boundary-clean locus; exact recovery follows from D1/F2, with the Hessian calculation retained as an independent regression.
supersedes: six-element coarse orbit as the degree-five separation theorem

entries.title: Degree-five rank-two regression
entries.scope: Generated degree-five specialization and independent checker for R1.

entries.title: Filtered A_2 quantization obstruction
entries.scope: The displayed degree-five quantization ansatz has the proved hbar^5 obstruction.

entries.title: Exact symplectic and Weyl lifts
entries.scope: proved for every characteristic-zero Keller map: the polynomial cotangent lift and inverse-Jacobian Weyl embedding preserve fibers and generic degree; differential-order-preserving Weyl equivalence descends to polynomial cotangent equivalence

entries.title: SU(2) and BCW consequence reproductions
entries.scope: locally proved and exactly replayed: the SU(2) Haar witness, the 3-to-39-to-79 route, and the repository 21-variable essential quotient yielding the route to not GMC(42)
dependencies: external: Bass--Connell--Wright reduction and Long's formulas
supersedes: conditional local reproduction of the SU(2) and BCW routes

entries.title: Fixed-dimensional GMC to SIC to Keller invertibility
entries.scope: proved for every fixed r; applied contrapositively to the 21-variable cubic collision to obtain a locally complete route to not GMC(42)
dependencies: external: Derksen--van den Essen--Zhao and Zhao implication architecture

entries.title: Direct Image and Vanishing counterexamples
entries.scope: proved by explicit identity-slice constructions: not SIC(20), generalized Laplacian/HN failure in 40 variables, homogeneous quartic HN-VC failure in 42 variables, and an all-order inverse recurrence
supersedes: 95-to-190 stable-normal-form dimensions for Image and Vanishing witnesses

entries.title: Constant-term Gaussian--Lagrange identity
entries.scope: proved coefficientwise for polynomial Phi with no restriction on Phi(0), including completed-ring, residue, determinant, and Wick normalization

entries.title: Weighted-seed Gaussian bridge and infinite-sequence injectivity
entries.scope: proved for every nonconstant normalized seed, producing explicit four-real-Gaussian GMC counterexamples; the complete mixed-moment sequence injectively determines the bridge polynomial h, and for nonzero scale injectively determines the normalized pair (H, lambda)
supersedes: bounded Wick-moment evidence for the weighted bridge

entries.title: Finite and optimal Gaussian mixed-moment fingerprints
entries.scope: For degree at most d, M_1 through M_(d+1) give finite recovery. On the normalized exact-degree-N slice at fixed nonzero scale, M_3 through M_(N-1) are global triangular coordinates, so exactly N-3 scalar moments are uniformly and generically minimal; variable scale requires exactly N-2. The resulting (N-3)-dimensional finitely fingerprinted families, fixed-support sparse slices, and explicit Toeplitz-Hessenberg moment equations are also proved.
supersedes: N+1 mixed-moment bound on the normalized seed slice

entries.title: Exceptional partition geometry in Gaussian moment space
entries.scope: At every fixed nonzero Gaussian scale and degree N >= 4, the optimal mixed-moment vector transports the full affine nonsurjective-seed stratification inside an explicit closed family of four-real-Gaussian GMC witnesses. Component counts, dimension staircases, normalizations, common-coarsening intersections, the generic 2^t transverse algebras, and the proved degree-eighteen ordered-triple algebra are preserved scheme-theoretically. In degree six the two components, four all-six intersection points, and their length-two local intersections have explicit moment-coordinate equations. Projective closure degrees, the open degree-eighteen conductor equalizer, and Gaussian-equivalence claims are excluded.

entries.title: Gaussian invariant-transport theorem
entries.scope: At fixed nonzero scale, the optimal N-3 moment isomorphism transports every functorially defined seed locus in its native category: schemes and formal neighborhoods, constructible sets, real semialgebraic chambers and boundaries, and arithmetic conditions at good reductions. In degree six the two vertical Ritt loci become explicit sextic moment hypersurfaces among four-real-Gaussian GMC witnesses; the 2-o-3 locus coincides exactly with the all-double nonsurjective component, and the Ritt intersection has an explicit clean rational point.
supersedes: partition-only interpretation of the Gaussian moment bridge

entries.title: Weighted invariant-coordinate Jacobian reduction
entries.scope: proved for arbitrary weight pattern (1,-1,-k), reducing the three-variable Jacobian to a two-invariant determinant

entries.title: Weighted tangent suspension and Poisson-square reduction
entries.scope: The stated plane-core setup, including the foundational pair, tangent pencil, and weighted-Wronskian cascade.

entries.title: Foundational weighted coefficient scheme
entries.scope: The foundational weighted coefficient scheme is computed in the stated bounded ansatz.

entries.title: Determinant-gauge source contact profile
entries.scope: Exact source-only filtered profile b_m=34m+1 in the determinant-normalized target gauge; this is not target-minimal and is not a stable LR obstruction.
supersedes: unfiltered infinite contact as a moduli invariant

entries.title: Torus-gauge associated-graded candidate
entries.scope: The class v^(6m)S^(4m) survives the invariant-ring-saturated equivariant target quotient in every order; its residue has v-degree at least 10m and hence detects ordinary source degree at least 20m up to fixed vector-basis shifts. The exact torus-gauge profile 24m+1 is supporting data, not an intrinsic minimum.

entries.title: Order-two weight-zero LR obstruction
entries.scope: In the ordinary-degree-25 homogeneous torus-weight-zero source sector, the kernel of the saturated target quotient is exactly R_24 times (x,-y,-2z). For every representative N(x,0,-3z)+P(x,-y,-2z), the order-two quadratic reconstruction has nonzero residue in R/(gamma); the coefficient ideal is the unit ideal. Rees strictness outside this face is not asserted.

entries.title: Quadratic ladder and rank-two Poisson completion
entries.scope: The quadratic ladder is the m=1 cancellation column, and the stated rank-two Poisson completion is exact.
supersedes: existence gap left by the incomplete rank-two Poisson card

entries.title: Degreewise exact symplectic and filtered Weyl stable moduli
entries.scope: proved for every generic degree N >= 4: (N-3)-dimensional families of stable exact symplectic classes in A^6 and stable differential-order-preserving Weyl left-right classes in A_3, with exact seed recovery on the compatible affine-marked open; no arbitrary-Weyl inequivalence claim

entries.title: Stable normal-form consequences
entries.scope: proved with corrected stable--Segre terminology: explicit 95-variable cubic and 451-variable Druzkowski reductions, plus valid 190-variable Image and HN witnesses
dependencies: external: Campbell and Gorni--Zampieri stable reductions
supersedes: polynomial-stable label for the Segre step

entries.title: Marked-point dimension barrier
entries.scope: proved for the complete marked-point/hyperplane-complement construction on P^1; affine-space source is possible only in dimension three
supersedes: unqualified extension of the marked-point construction to dimension two

entries.title: Relative-scaling boundary-lattice theorem
entries.scope: proved for two-factor normalization torsors and multi-factor boundary complements, including units, Picard torsion, canonical classes, and the multi-factor unit obstruction
supersedes: separate scaling, unit, and Picard calculations

entries.title: Consecutive-factor contact reduction
entries.scope: A finite motivic classification algorithm for every consecutive row; the quadratic--cubic row is classified and the cubic--quartic row has no affine-space exception.
supersedes: quadratic--cubic row as the only tested consecutive-factor obstruction

entries.title: Normalized quadratic--cubic factorization slice
entries.scope: The normalized factorization slice has the proved smoothness, factoriality, motivic, Chow, K-theoretic, and homotopy invariants.
supersedes: affine-five-space candidate for the tangent quadratic--cubic slice

entries.title: Plane Jacobian degree frontier
entries.scope: locally reproduced conditional on the published minimal/standard normal-form reductions: any plane counterexample has larger coordinate degree at least 125; this does not prove JC(2) or attainability
dependencies: external: published minimal-standard-pair reductions and exact (72,108) certificates

entries.title: Dicritical boundary classification
entries.scope: proved for every admissible primitive in characteristic zero: all dicritical divisors of the normalized inverse graph and their target images are classified

entries.title: Higher cancellation contact resultants
entries.scope: Complete r=6 by making its 29-branch asymptotic threshold effective and certifying the finite remainder, then prove the residual wedge r>=7 not covered by parameter irreducibility. Any unresolved pair has m>=7, composite N=(m+1)r+1, and at most one prime in (mr,N).

entries.title: Linear target-lift Rees strictness
entries.scope: For every relevant filtered face, construct the target-field modules weight by weight, their lifted initial submodule in h_F, and the normal quotient, and prove that taking the initial face commutes with the linear target lift. Treat this as a finite module/SAGBI problem, not a further target-jet expansion; the same presentation should expose the finite set of weights used by the quadratic calculation.

entries.title: Quadratic target-lift LR symbol classification
entries.scope: The minimal pair p=1 is already nonzero as O8, so quadratic strict descent fails. Compute the remaining generator matrices for II_(F,p,-p), prove a structural finite-weight cutoff, and determine whether the minimal class generates the full quadratic normal image.

entries.title: Marked-cover valuative no escape
entries.scope: Construct the proper closure of the LR-induced Isom relation first on marked normalized incidence covers. Use exact recovery from the marked Hessian divisor and affine sheet, unique coarse specialization at every DVR collision, and generic deck rigidity to prove that this marked Isom object is finite and proper on the clean locus; extend over a Hurwitz/admissible-cover compactification and reconstruct the LR equivalence only afterwards.

entries.title: Hessian-Ritt reduction and degree-six incidence classification
entries.scope: Vertical (a,b)-decomposability is the pullback of the projective Hessian-composition incidence and is finite etale over that incidence on the D1/F2 clean open. Its codimension is at least N-a-b, proving that generic seeds in every composite degree N>=6 have no imprimitive vertical specialization. In degree six the 3-o-2 and 2-o-3 incidences are explicit cubic and quartic hypersurfaces of exact expected codimension one; their intersection is exactly the monomial/Chebyshev Ritt collision, its normalized marked seed curve and clean-boundary exclusions are explicit, and a rational clean witness is given.
supersedes: degree-six full coefficient elimination for OP-RITT

entries.title: All-degree Hessian atlas and degree-eight/twelve Ritt diagrams
entries.scope: For every factor pair ab=N, canonical monic coefficient reconstruction makes the Hessian-composition incidence rational of exact codimension N-a-b. In degree eight the two factor-order loci meet in the irreducible excess 2-o-2-o-2 refinement locus of marked dimension two. In degree twelve all six pair intersections and all higher intersections are classified: three common-refinement components, two transported degree-six Ritt components, power and Chebyshev components for 3-o-4 versus 4-o-3, and the all-four intersection is exactly the Chebyshev component. Every component meets the D1/F2 clean open.
supersedes: generic-finiteness and expected-codimension part of OP-RITT

entries.title: Vertical imprimitive-specialization loci
entries.scope: Extend the explicit Ritt-intersection diagrams beyond degree twelve using the proved all-degree canonical Hessian atlas, and restrict the marked-root/Hessian collision equations to those incidence components to classify the H3 boundary degenerations of the distinguished affine sheet.

entries.title: Cancellation arithmetic
entries.scope: Irreducibility and Galois-group questions for the cancellation parameter polynomials.

entries.title: Controlled-boundary suspension classification
entries.scope: Classify controlled-boundary suspensions beyond the canonical constructions.

entries.title: Wider quantization lifts
entries.scope: Search beyond the bounded quantization ansatz after the proved hbar^5 obstruction.

entries.title: Coefficient-scheme boundary gluing
entries.scope: Determine the nilpotent boundary gluing of the proved foundational coefficient scheme.

entries.title: Factorization-slice product and flexibility
entries.scope: Decide the global A^2 x SL_2 product and flexibility questions.

entries.title: Poisson-completion provenance
entries.scope: Resolve the provenance of the announced external rank-two Poisson manuscript.

entries.title: Plane-Jacobian degree frontier
entries.scope: Advance the plane-Jacobian degree frontier beyond the reproduced certified ranges.

entries.title: Gaussian and Image/Vanishing consequences
entries.scope: Determine which mixed-moment or transported exceptional invariants descend under natural equivalence groups of Gaussian polynomial pairs, and whether the weighted bridge admits a uniform three-real-variable realization beyond the proved separated-quadratic obstruction.
